title: Anthropic negotiates with Samsung for the production of its own artificial intelligence chip
summary: The company seeks to reduce its dependence on Nvidia by developing customized hardware alongside the Korean manufacturer.

title: The Steam Machine platform resurges with new hardware, open source, and technical controversies
summary: Valve publishes the design of its e-ink screen, Aoostar launches a clone, and critical failures are reported in some units.

title: Sony gradually eliminates physical format games for PlayStation
summary: The company justifies the measure by cost savings, while the community reacts negatively and GitHub launches repository CDs as a sign of protest.

title: Meta admits that its AI agents are not advancing as expected and acknowledges that layoffs were ineffective
summary: Zuckerberg's company changes its strategy to sell computing capacity instead of just buying it, adjusting its staff and priorities.

title: AI demand skyrockets DRAM and NAND memory prices by up to 18%
summary: Manufacturers increase investments and pressure the US government, while chip prices become more expensive.

title: Anthropic perfects Claude Code while Alibaba bans it for alleged security risks
summary: The AI coding tool reduces its initial prompt and enters labs, but faces blocks due to alleged backdoors.

title: Intel increases the price of its high-end Core Ultra 7 and 5 processors
summary: The company applies an unannounced increase to its most powerful desktop chips.

title: Intel overcomes performance issues of its 18A process and doubles production
summary: Manufacturing of future chips reaches 30,000 monthly wafers after fixing defects.

title: EU Court upholds €4.1 billion fine on Google for Android abuse
summary: The penalty for anti-competitive practices is confirmed after years of litigation.

title: MEP investigating Pegasus spyware hacked with the same program
summary: The attack demonstrates the vulnerability even of those who pursue cyberespionage.

title: OpenAI offers 5% of its capital to a US sovereign wealth fund
summary: The proposal seeks to align the company's interests with those of the US government.

title: TV show tracking app TV Time will close in 2026 and focus on AI
summary: The service recommends alternatives to its users before the final shutdown.

title: A flaw in Apple's Hide My Email exposes users' real addresses
summary: The vulnerability allows attackers to discover emails hidden by the privacy feature.

title: Podman 6.0 improves container integration with modern networks and pod management
summary: The new version of the daemonless container engine simplifies development and administration.

title: Microsoft plans an operating system with integrated Copilot called Project Aion
summary: A leaked video shows the company's plans to deepen AI in Windows.

title: Philips introduces gaming monitors with triple mode and refresh rate up to 540 Hz
summary: The new models allow switching between QHD, FHD, and HD resolutions without losing fluidity.

title: Xbox prepares for the end of physical discs with new conversion feature and changes to Game Pass
summary: Microsoft is working on a system to transform physical games into digital and pauses the addition of new titles to the catalog.

title: Nvidia offers to finance AI infrastructure in exchange for a percentage of cloud revenue
summary: The company proposes an alternative payment model that includes tokens and a share of the client's billing.

title: Microsoft invests $2.5 billion in a company to integrate AI into corporate clients
summary: The initiative will place 6,000 AI engineers directly into companies.

title: Tesla deliveries increase 25% to nearly 500,000 vehicles
summary: The company exceeds market expectations despite economic challenges.
